heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 103–272, § 7(b)108 Stat. 1379July 5, 1994
label: 81 to 92 Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 103–272, § 7(b)108 Stat. 1379July 5, 1994
notes: Section 81, R.S. § 5256; , , , related to Union Pacific Railroad Company, its books, records, correspondence, other documents, dividends, stock issuance, mortgages or pledges, and directors or officers.Nov. 6, 1978Pub. L. 95–598, title III, § 322(g)92 Stat. 2679
Section 82, R.S. § 5257, provided for connection of other roads with Union Pacific Railroad or any of its branches.

Section 83, acts , ; , ; , , provided that companies authorized by Pacific Railroad Acts to construct railroads were required to operate roads and telegraphs as one continuous line and to afford equal advantages and facilities as to rates, time, and transportation to the other companies without discrimination.July 2, 1864, ch. 216, § 1513 Stat. 362June 20, 1874, ch. 33118 Stat. 111Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 29136 Stat. 1167
Section 84, R.S. § 5258, authorized interstate transport by railroads.

Section 85, R.S. § 5259, related to compensation of directors, engineers, commissioners, or other agents appointed by the United States to examine roads or to act in conjunction with other officers of railroads or other corporations receiving land grants or other subsidies.
Section 86, R.S. § 5260, related to withholding by Secretary of the Treasury of payments to certain railroads.

Section 87, R.S. § 5261; , ; , , ; , , , provided that companies from whom payments for freight and transportation had been withheld under provisions of could sue in Court of Federal Claims.June 25, 1948, ch. 646, § 462 Stat. 986Pub. L. 97–164, title I, § 161(8)96 Stat. 49Pub. L. 102–572, title IX, § 902(b)(2)106 Stat. 4516section 86 of this titleApr. 2, 1982Oct. 29, 1992

Section 88, R.S. § 5262; , , related to jurisdiction of proper United States district court to hear and determine all cases of mandamus to compel Union Pacific Railroad Company to operate its road.Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 29136 Stat. 1167

Section 89, acts , ; , , directed Secretary of the Treasury to require that railroad companies, their successors and assigns, pay United States a percentage of net earnings as provided for by law.June 22, 1874, ch. 41418 Stat. 200Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 29136 Stat. 1167

Section 90, , , authorized Secretary of the Treasury to settle accounts of Union Pacific, Central Pacific, Kansas Pacific, Western Pacific, and Sioux City and Pacific Railroad Companies for services provided to Government in transporting the Army and mails.act Mar. 3, 1879, ch. 183, § 120 Stat. 420
Section 91, , , provided that provisions of were extended and made applicable to Navy and Marine Corps transportation.act Mar. 3, 1897, ch. 38629 Stat. 663section 90 of this title
Section 92, , , authorized and directed Secretary of the Treasury to settle claims stemming from Government transportation over non-bond-aided lines.act Mar. 3, 1901, ch. 831, § 131 Stat. 1023
